headline: 101°F and 2 days dry — water and tip canes.
weather_notes: Run both zones early this morning — 2 days dry entering 101°F crosses the 1-day re-water threshold for heat above 85°F.
Finish all garden work before 8 AM — heat stays 96–101°F through Thursday with no meaningful rain in the forecast.
Hang shade cloth over e1–e4 by midday — 24-day transplants risk sunscald at 101°F; remove by 5 PM.
Skip foliar sprays through Tuesday night — 13% storm chance could wash off same-day applications; Wednesday morning is the first clean window.

surfaced_tasks.title: Tip new blackberry primocanes if 3–4 ft tall
surfaced_tasks.reason: 4 days overdue — the Extreme Heat Warning lifts at 6 AM this morning, opening the first workable early window since Jul 10.

suggested_tasks.title: Pick ripe blackberries before midday heat
suggested_tasks.description: Berries observed Jun 14 are 30 days into peak season — harvest any ripe fruit this morning before 101°F softens and damages them on the cane.

suggested_tasks.title: First feed e1–e4 tomatoes with fish emulsion
suggested_tasks.description: 24-day transplants have no fertilizer logged — apply fish emulsion Wednesday morning, the first clear window before Thursday's 20% storm chance.
